sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: A+, Moody's: A1, Fitch: A

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Japan faces significant exposure to the oil price surge given its 10% energy independence index -- the second-lowest among major economies after Taiwan. The LDP's supermajority (won Feb 8) provides stable governance under PM Ishiba. Defense spending reached a record $58B with Type-12 long-range missile deployment, and the government stated SDF could intervene if China acts militarily on Taiwan. BOJ policy normalization continues. Inflation at 3.6% remains above target.
key_changes_this_week: Oil +13% surge creates acute import cost pressure (energy independence: 10%) (warning)
Record $58B defense budget with Type-12 missile deployment at Camp Kengun (moderate)
Government stated SDF could intervene if China acts militarily on Taiwan
LDP supermajority provides legislative stability for Ishiba government
narrative.outlook: Japan's near-term risk is dominated by energy import cost exposure to the Hormuz crisis. Extended Hormuz disruption could widen the current account deficit (-4.3% GDP) and reignite yen weakness. The BOJ's policy normalization path may be complicated by oil-driven inflation. Defense spending trajectory is structurally upward. Japan's strategic positioning on Taiwan represents a significant shift in post-WWII defense posture.
narrative.investor_implications: JPY likely weakens if oil stays elevated; yen-funded carry trades may unwind. Nikkei faces headwinds from energy costs but benefits from yen weakness for exporters. Defense-related equities (MHI, KHI, IHI) are structural beneficiaries. JGB yields may face upward pressure from inflation. Monitor BOJ March meeting closely.
narrative.data_quality_note: Japan macro data is high quality from BOJ and government sources. Defense spending confirmed in FY2026 budget. Energy independence index is a derived metric.

active_alerts.title: Japan highly exposed to oil price surge (energy independence: 10%)
active_alerts.description: Oil prices surged 13% following the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse. Japan has an energy independence index of only 0.1, making it highly vulnerable to sustained energy price increases. Brent crude forecast to spike to $100/barrel if conflict persists. This threatens inflation, trade balance, and GDP growth for energy-import-dependent economies.

active_alerts.title: Japan LDP wins supermajority enabling constitutional revision; defense budget $58B
active_alerts.description: Japan's LDP won a historic supermajority in Feb 8 snap election, enabling potential revision of the pacifist constitution. Defense budget rising to $58B with strong_growth trend in military expenditure. PM Takaichi's conservative agenda may affect trade relations with China. High chokepoint exposure (0.53) increases vulnerability to Hormuz disruption.

economy.sovereign_credit_rating: S&P: A+, Moody's: A1, Fitch: A
